name: Caesar Salad
recipeInstructions:
Zesty Tabasco and Worcestershire spice up this innovation from "City Cuisine" by Susan Feniger and Mary Sue Milliken (William Morrow and Co., 1989). The dressing contains lightly cooked egg, so if made ahead must be refrigerated.
Combine anchovies, black pepper, and olive oil in a blender. Puree for about 5 minutes until very smooth. Add grated Parmesan cheese. Measure and reserve 1/3 cup for use with croutons.
Bring a small saucepan of water to a boil. Place a refrigerated egg on a slotted spoon into the boiling water. Cook for 1 1/2 minutes; remove and reserve.
Place vinegar, lemon juice, mustard, celery salt, Tabasco sauce, and Worcestershire sauce in a large bowl. Add anchovy mixture. Crack open the egg (including any uncooked parts) and whisk into the mixture until well combined. Refrigerate dressing if not using right away. Combine reserved anchovy mixture with diced bread; toss to coat. Heat a dry cast-iron skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until golden and crisp.
Wash and dry lettuce. Break into bite-sized pieces. Place in a salad bowl along with dressing. Toss well. Add toasted croutons and toss again before serving.
Makes 6 servings.
Notes:
To crack peppercorns, place whole peppercorns on a work surface. Place the bottom of a heavy skillet or saucepan on top and press down firmly.
To puree garlic, place peeled garlic cloves in a blender with a dash of olive oil; puree. Make in quantity if desired, and store refrigerated for up to two weeks.
